title: AF phase mapping and probabilistic rotor localization
expected_band: from sparse noisy electrodes the phase field of a reaction-diffusion spiral is reconstructed and the rotor core is localized as a probability heatmap with a confidence radius, rather than a single point

ladder.classical: Hilbert-transform phase + phase-singularity detection returning a single rotor point (noise-sensitive)
ladder.sota: physics-informed EP models of fibrillation (EP-PINNs) and learned rotor/phase mapping (deep networks)
ladder.novel: an uncertainty-aware rotor map: a probabilistic phase-singularity heatmap + a confidence radius from sparse noisy electrodes, not a single point

engine.model: Aliev-Panfilov reaction-diffusion spiral; complex-phasor reconstruction from sparse electrodes; ensemble probabilistic rotor map

reasons: not web-drivable (input is not a browser-suppliable coordinate/parameter)

references.cite: Herrero Martin et al. (2022). EP-PINNs: cardiac electrophysiology parameter estimation. Frontiers in Cardiovascular Medicine 8:768419
Lebert, Christoph et al. (2021). Rotor localization and phase mapping of cardiac excitation waves using deep neural networks. Frontiers in Physiology 12:782176
Aliev, Panfilov (1996). A simple two-variable model of cardiac excitation. Chaos, Solitons & Fractals 7(3)
